description: Water moving across land or a built surface. It can carry sediment, warmth, or nothing worth noting, and its presence alone proves neither pollution nor where any pollution came from.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'wi' glides into bright 'i', the first quick movement of water over a surface. Across open 'a', stressed 'ra' rolls into the wet resonance of 'wirua' (wet). Liquid 'lu' carries the sound downhill into deep 'u'. Uninterrupted movement gathers speed and traces water along the fall of the land.
grammatical_notes: A mass or countable noun: 'ha wiralu lue ha kosha wea ha luphore selu.' (This runoff flows from the field toward the river.) State source, surface, timing, carried material, and destination separately. Distinct from 'pheralu' (rain), 'selu' (flow), stream water, wastewater, and 'pesenu' (pollute).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Runoff makes the material consequences of fields, roofs, paths, and streets traceable without presuming either contamination or blame.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reading surface water links soil, slope, cultivation, settlement, and downstream conditions in practical land knowledge.

semantic_domains.nature: water moving across land or built surfaces
semantic_domains.physical: a mobile body of surface water that may carry material
semantic_domains.spatial: movement from a source surface toward a lower or receiving place
